sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I have clear positions:
- Player (M): boundary starts at [36, 11] - so the player is at rows 36-37, cols 11-15 (approximately)
- R block: [19, 18] to [22, 21] - rows 19-22, cols 18-21
- b block: [25, 18] to [28, 21] - rows 25-28, cols 18-21
- N block: [31, 18] to [34, 21] - rows 31-34, cols 18-21
The player is at row 36-37, which is BELOW the N block (rows 31-34).
The narrow panel is at cols 13-14 (the G blocks).
The dark gray panel is at cols 17-46, rows 12-41.
The bottom panel shows: M at [56, 20]-[57, 25], R at [57, 27]-[60, 30], N at [57, 33]-[60, 36], b at [57, 39]-[60, 42].
So the bottom panel arrangement is: M, R, N, b (left to right).
The main board arrangement is: R (top), b (middle), N (bottom).
The goal might be to rearrange the blocks on the main board to match the bottom panel's horizontal order: R, N, b.
But how do I move blocks? Let me think about this differently.
What if the player needs to "pick up" blocks by touching them with the path, then "place" them somewhere else?
Currently the player is at the bottom. Let me try extending RIGHT to touch the N block, then moving the player to a different position to see if the block moves with the player.
Actually, let me first go back to the top of the panel and start fresh. The player needs to be at the top to collect blocks in the correct order.
Wait, but I already know that extending RIGHT from the top and touching blocks doesn't make them disappear. The blocks stay on the board. So "collecting" isn't the right term.
Let me try a completely different approach: what if I need to move the player to the RIGHT side of the dark gray panel and then move UP/DOWN to "sweep" the blocks into a new arrangement?
Actually, let me try something simple: move the player UP to the top, then extend RIGHT all the way to the right edge, then move DOWN along the right edge. This would create a path that goes around the blocks. Maybe this "sweeps" them into a new arrangement?
Let me first move the player UP to the top.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move player UP to the top of the narrow panel
# Player is at row 36-37, top of panel is around row 12-15
# Need about 20 UP moves
action(['UP'] * 20)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
